name: AlphaGenome
provider_access: provider-controlled access to the AlphaGenome service
license_summary: custom provider terms; access is gated by Google/DeepMind service controls
redistribution_summary: do not assume open redistribution rights for returned prediction outputs
notes: BioMCP calls the hosted gRPC endpoint directly. The public product page is the closest official access reference currently exposed.

name: cBioPortal
provider_access: public API access for open studies
license_summary: public API with study-specific downstream terms
redistribution_summary: reuse depends on the specific study or consortium behind each dataset
notes: BioMCP uses the public API. TCGA-derived studies are broadly open, while some cohorts such as AACR Project GENIE carry additional access or reuse conditions.

name: ChEMBL
license_summary: EMBL-EBI open data service; ChEMBL is published for broad reuse
redistribution_summary: reuse is generally allowed under the provider's open-data terms with attribution where required
notes: BioMCP only queries live ChEMBL endpoints and does not ship ChEMBL data in the repository.

name: CIViC
license_summary: open community knowledgebase; CIViC content is published for unrestricted reuse
redistribution_summary: reuse is broadly permitted; attribution remains best practice
notes: CIViC is treated here as an open-access evidence source surfaced directly by BioMCP.

name: ClinGen
license_summary: public ClinGen curation resources with publication and attribution expectations
redistribution_summary: generally queryable and reusable, but users should preserve attribution and source context
notes: Open Targets currently lists ClinGen under CC0 for its own ingestion, but BioMCP links to ClinGen's official project site because that is the provider surface users encounter directly.

name: ClinicalTrials.gov
license_summary: U.S. government public information service
redistribution_summary: records are broadly reusable; preserve identifiers and avoid implying NLM endorsement
notes: BioMCP uses the public v2 API as the baseline trial backend.

name: ComplexPortal
license_summary: EMBL-EBI open data service
redistribution_summary: reuse follows EMBL-EBI resource terms and any embedded third-party source obligations
notes: BioMCP calls the IntAct Complex Portal web service. Complex membership data is queried on demand only.

name: CPIC
license_summary: CPIC content is published under CC0 with trademark and attribution guidance
redistribution_summary: content reuse is broadly allowed, but the CPIC mark/logo has separate restrictions
notes: CPIC announced in March 2026 that content is moving to ClinPGx, but current CPIC URLs continue to resolve.

name: DGIdb
license_summary: open interaction service; aggregated claims may still reflect upstream source terms
redistribution_summary: treat DGIdb as an aggregation layer and preserve source attribution for underlying claim providers
notes: DGIdb itself is open to query, but it aggregates claims from many external drug-gene sources.

name: DisGeNET
provider_access: account registration and API key required
license_summary: custom provider terms for API and downloads
redistribution_summary: do not assume unrestricted redistribution; use according to the provider account terms
notes: DisGeNET's public site advertises free and commercial plans. BioMCP documents the API as key-gated and treats it as provider-controlled.

name: Enrichr
license_summary: open web/API service with citation expectations for Enrichr and its libraries
redistribution_summary: reuse of results should preserve attribution to Enrichr and the underlying enrichment libraries
notes: Gene enrichment sections inside BioMCP use Enrichr; top-level `biomcp enrich` uses g:Profiler instead.

name: Europe PMC
license_summary: open literature metadata service; article and full-text licenses vary by record
redistribution_summary: metadata is broadly reusable, but full text and PDFs remain governed by article-level licenses
notes: BioMCP uses Europe PMC for search and bibliographic metadata. Open-access reuse depends on the publication license attached to each record.

name: gnomAD
license_summary: Broad Institute data policies with attribution and service-specific conditions
redistribution_summary: querying is open, but users should review the gnomAD policies before bulk reuse or republishing
notes: Open Targets currently reports gnomAD as CC0 for its own ingestion pipeline. BioMCP links to gnomAD's own policy page for user-facing guidance.

name: g:Profiler
license_summary: open enrichment service with provider citation expectations
redistribution_summary: results are queryable and reusable, but cite g:Profiler and any underlying databases you depend on
notes: BioMCP uses g:Profiler only for top-level gene-set enrichment.

name: GTEx
provider_access: open public portal/API for public-access expression data
license_summary: NIH-hosted public-access expression resource
redistribution_summary: public summary/expression views are broadly reusable; controlled-access data remains outside BioMCP's scope
notes: BioMCP only queries public GTEx expression endpoints, not controlled-access donor-level data.

name: GWAS Catalog
license_summary: EMBL-EBI resource terms; summary statistics may carry separate licenses
redistribution_summary: query results are generally reusable, but dataset-level summary statistics can have separate downstream terms
notes: Open Targets reports GWAS Catalog summary statistics under CC0 while the service remains under EMBL-EBI terms.

name: Human Protein Atlas
provider_access: open public XML and web resources
license_summary: CC BY-SA 4.0 for copyrightable parts of the database
redistribution_summary: reuse is allowed with attribution and ShareAlike; third-party components may impose extra conditions
notes: The licence page also requires clear citation for images and specific gene/data pages.

name: HPO JAX API
license_summary: open HPO data with attribution and integrity requirements
redistribution_summary: reuse is allowed, but users should preserve attribution, version context, and source integrity
notes: The HPO project's own license page is more specific than generic JAX site text and is the clearest official usage statement currently exposed.

name: InterPro
license_summary: EMBL-EBI open data resource
redistribution_summary: reuse follows InterPro/EMBL-EBI resource terms and any embedded member-database obligations
notes: InterPro aggregates signatures from multiple member databases; downstream interpretation should keep that provenance.

name: KEGG
provider_access: open website/API for academic use; commercial use requires a license
license_summary: custom KEGG terms; academic users may freely use the website, non-academic use requires a commercial license
redistribution_summary: do not assume commercial redistribution rights; query access does not grant a redistribution license
notes: KEGG's official legal page was updated on October 1, 2024 and explicitly distinguishes academic from non-academic use.

name: MedlinePlus
license_summary: NLM public-information service with trademark and endorsement guidance
redistribution_summary: content is widely reusable, but preserve attribution and avoid implying MedlinePlus/NLM endorsement
notes: BioMCP uses MedlinePlus only for best-effort plain-language discover context.

name: Monarch Initiative
license_summary: open integrated knowledge graph; underlying source licenses still matter
redistribution_summary: results can be queried openly, but downstream reuse should respect the original sources folded into Monarch
notes: BioMCP uses Monarch as an aggregator for disease, phenotype, and model-organism relationships rather than as the legal source of every embedded assertion.

name: MyChem.info
license_summary: BioThings aggregation service; upstream source terms continue to apply
redistribution_summary: do not assume aggregator responses are relicensed; preserve source provenance for downstream reuse
notes: DrugBank and other upstream providers appear in MyChem payloads with their own terms.

name: MyDisease.info
license_summary: BioThings aggregation service; source ontologies and datasets keep their own terms
redistribution_summary: treat payloads as aggregated source data rather than a new umbrella license
notes: Disease Ontology and MONDO appear through MyDisease.info as indirect provenance sources.

name: MyGene.info
license_summary: BioThings aggregation service; source-specific terms remain attached to underlying records
redistribution_summary: reuse should preserve provenance back to NCBI Gene, UniProt, and other upstream sources
notes: BioMCP uses MyGene.info mainly as an identity/normalization layer rather than as the legal origin of all gene data.

name: MyVariant.info
license_summary: BioThings aggregation service; indirect providers retain their own terms
redistribution_summary: ClinVar, COSMIC, Cancer Genome Interpreter, and gnomAD-related fields should be treated according to their original providers' terms
notes: BioMCP intentionally documents several indirect-only provenance rows that arrive through MyVariant.info payloads.

name: NCBI ID Converter
provider_access: open public utility; optional My NCBI API key improves throughput
license_summary: NLM public-domain utility service
redistribution_summary: utility results are broadly reusable; keep article-level identifiers and downstream article licenses distinct
notes: NCBI API keys increase throughput but are not required for baseline BioMCP usage.

name: NCI CTS
license_summary: custom provider API terms for the NCI Clinical Trials Search API
redistribution_summary: query output is usable for search and review, but downstream reuse should follow NCI API terms and record provenance
notes: BioMCP treats NCI CTS as an alternate oncology-focused trial backend, not the default public trial source.

name: OLS4
license_summary: EMBL-EBI ontology browser; each ontology keeps its own license
redistribution_summary: ontology metadata is queryable, but downstream reuse depends on the specific ontology surfaced
notes: OLS4 is BioMCP's required backbone for discover. It is an ontology index, not a single-license data source.

name: OncoKB
license_summary: custom provider terms; academic research access is no-fee but licensed, commercial/clinical use requires a paid license
redistribution_summary: do not assume open redistribution rights for OncoKB data or proprietary treatment descriptions
notes: The FAQ states that programmatic access for academic use still requires registration and provider approval.

name: OpenFDA
provider_access: open public API; optional key increases quota headroom
license_summary: FDA-origin public data and API terms
redistribution_summary: data is broadly reusable, but avoid implying FDA endorsement and preserve source context
notes: The authentication page says API keys are required while also publishing no-key quotas. BioMCP documents the real runtime behavior: baseline use works without a key, with higher quotas when one is configured.

name: OpenTargets
license_summary: Open Targets data is CC0; platform code is Apache 2.0
redistribution_summary: platform data is dedicated to the public domain, but linked evidence still carries source provenance
notes: The licence page also lists the licensing status of major upstream datasets consumed by Open Targets.

name: PharmGKB
license_summary: ClinPGx API data is CC BY-SA 4.0 and subject to the provider's data usage policy
redistribution_summary: reuse is allowed with attribution and ShareAlike; some underlying annotations and external assets may add extra constraints
notes: PharmGKB has been transitioning to ClinPGx-branded API documentation. BioMCP keeps the public `PharmGKB` source label because that is the domain vocabulary users recognize.

name: PMC OA
provider_access: open public utility for the PMC Open Access subset
license_summary: open-access subset only; article licenses vary within PMC OA
redistribution_summary: full text is reusable only according to each article's specific PMC Open Access license
notes: BioMCP queries PMC OA on demand and does not ship the article corpus. Returned full text is still governed by article-level licenses.

name: PubTator3
provider_access: open public API; optional My NCBI API key improves throughput
license_summary: NCBI/NLM public-domain annotation service
redistribution_summary: results are broadly reusable, but preserve PMID/source provenance and article-level rights separately
notes: BioMCP uses PubTator3 for article search fan-out and article annotation. `NCBI_API_KEY` is optional quota uplift only.

name: QuickGO
license_summary: GO/EMBL-EBI open data service
redistribution_summary: query results are generally reusable; preserve GO/EMBL-EBI attribution where expected
notes: QuickGO exposes GO data and annotations; some embedded evidence sources can carry their own provenance requirements.

name: Reactome
license_summary: Reactome pathway content is CC BY 4.0, with some data exports additionally placed under CC0
redistribution_summary: reuse is allowed with attribution; preserve pathway/source provenance in downstream materials
notes: Reactome announced in 2017 that some annotation files moved to CC0 while core site/code materials remained under CC BY 4.0.

name: Semantic Scholar
provider_access: API key required for BioMCP's Semantic Scholar search/helper path and governed by the API license agreement
license_summary: custom API license agreement
redistribution_summary: the API license restricts repackaging, resale, and broad commercial redistribution without expanded licensing
notes: The overview page says many endpoints are publicly reachable without auth, but BioMCP's article search leg and helper commands deliberately require `S2_API_KEY` to stay within the supported quota path.

name: STRING
license_summary: CC BY 4.0
redistribution_summary: reuse is allowed with attribution to STRING and the original publication/resource
notes: BioMCP queries STRING network endpoints on demand and does not package STRING interaction datasets.

name: UMLS
provider_access: UTS account plus acceptance of the UMLS Metathesaurus license
license_summary: custom UMLS Metathesaurus license and terminology-specific appendices
redistribution_summary: do not assume unrestricted redistribution; some embedded vocabularies add their own restrictions or affiliate licenses
notes: The UMLS landing page explicitly states that you must accept the license and create a UTS account for access.

name: UniProt
license_summary: CC BY 4.0
redistribution_summary: reuse is allowed with attribution; linked cross-references can have their own terms
notes: BioMCP also surfaces UniProt cross-references to PDB and AlphaFold DB rather than mirroring those datasets directly.

name: WikiPathways
license_summary: CC0
redistribution_summary: pathway content is dedicated to the public domain; attribution is still good scholarly practice
notes: The current license statement is still hosted on the WikiPathways classic site.

name: AlphaFold DB
provider_access: surfaced through UniProt cross-references; no standalone BioMCP client
license_summary: AlphaFold DB structural predictions are published for broad open use
redistribution_summary: reuse is generally open, but preserve model/source provenance and article citations
notes: BioMCP does not call AlphaFold DB directly. Structure links appear through UniProt cross-references.

name: Cancer Genome Interpreter
license_summary: custom tool terms
redistribution_summary: do not assume commercial reuse rights; the official terms restrict some external and commercial use
notes: There is no standalone CGI source client in BioMCP; provenance appears only when MyVariant includes CGI fields.

name: ClinVar
license_summary: NCBI public-domain submission archive
redistribution_summary: records are broadly reusable, but preserve accession/provenance and submitter context
notes: BioMCP does not call ClinVar directly; ClinVar assertions arrive through MyVariant.info.

name: COSMIC
provider_access: surfaced indirectly through cached MyVariant.info fields; no standalone BioMCP COSMIC client
license_summary: custom COSMIC licensing with commercial restrictions
redistribution_summary: direct redistribution and direct integration remain intentionally unsupported without a separate COSMIC license
notes: This is the most important indirect-only caution row. BioMCP intentionally does not support direct COSMIC querying because of licensing risk.

name: Disease Ontology
license_summary: open disease ontology project
redistribution_summary: reuse is generally open; preserve ontology version and source references
notes: BioMCP does not maintain a standalone Disease Ontology client.

name: DrugBank
license_summary: custom DrugBank terms of use and licensing
redistribution_summary: use or redistribution of DrugBank content requires a DrugBank license; do not assume open downstream rights
notes: DrugBank does not have a standalone BioMCP source client. It appears as provenance carried through MyChem.info.

name: Drugs@FDA
license_summary: FDA-origin public information
redistribution_summary: approval records are broadly reusable; avoid implying FDA endorsement
notes: BioMCP documents Drugs@FDA as an indirect provenance label because approval fields arrive through OpenFDA, not a dedicated Drugs@FDA client.

name: MONDO
license_summary: CC BY 4.0
redistribution_summary: reuse is allowed with attribution and ontology version tracking
notes: BioMCP uses MONDO identifiers through MyDisease.info and other aggregators rather than calling MONDO directly.

name: PDB
provider_access: surfaced through UniProt cross-references; no standalone BioMCP PDB client
license_summary: PDB archive data is CC0 1.0
redistribution_summary: data is broadly reusable; attribution to original structure authors is encouraged
notes: BioMCP currently exposes PDB identifiers from UniProt rather than querying RCSB PDB directly.
